issue.title: Use generated request adapter for WP_Http::request stream blocking

artifacts.role: WPHX-312.72 runner installing compiler-emitted class-wp-http.php request shell
stock Haxe PHP compile anchor for request helper modules used by the generated shell
stream-blocking candidate manifest with generated-shell and IR feature evidence
ownership manifest marking the boundary as compiler_emitted_original_path_shell
behavior receipt for the stream-blocking candidate

claims: WPHX-312.72 now consumes the compiler-emitted original-path WP_Http::request shell instead of a copied/runner-patched class-wp-http.php body.
The stream-blocking helper remains the bounded Haxe-owned branch claim, with helper modules compiled for the generated shell's runtime calls.
The deterministic streaming oracle/candidate probe remains behavior-identical.
non_claims: This does not claim full WP_Http::request ownership.
This does not claim live streaming transfer behavior, network I/O, upstream HTTP PHPUnit parity, or installed distribution behavior.
This does not claim whole-file WP_Http ownership.
